obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that deploy subliminal techniques beyond a person's consciousness or purposefully manipulative or deceptive techniques, with the objective or with the effect of materially distorting the behaviour of a person or a group of persons, causing or reasonably likely to cause significant harm.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that exploit vulnerabilities due to age, disability or a specific social or economic situation, with the objective or with the effect of materially distorting the behaviour of a person, causing or reasonably likely to cause significant harm.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that evaluate or classify natural persons or groups based on social behaviour or personal or personality characteristics, where the social score leads to detrimental or unfavourable treatment in unrelated social contexts or to unjustified or disproportionate treatment relative to the gravity of the social behaviour.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union, whether provided or used by public or private persons

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that assess or predict the risk of a person committing a criminal offence solely based on profiling of a natural person or on assessing their personality traits and characteristics, except to support a human assessment based on objective and verifiable facts directly linked to a criminal activity.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that create or expand facial recognition databases through untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV footage.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems to infer emotions of natural persons at the workplace or in educational institutions, except for medical or safety reasons.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that individually categorise persons based on their biometric data to deduce or infer their race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious or philosophical beliefs, sex-life or sexual orientation, except for lawful labelling or filtering of lawfully acquired biometric datasets including in the area of law enforcement.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not use AI systems for real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes, except where necessary for the targeted search of specific victims, prevention of specific threats including terrorist attacks, or search of suspects of specific offences, and subject to the further procedural requirements including for authorisation outlined in Article 5(2)-(7) AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems used in the Union for law enforcement purposes

obligations.requirement_text: Providers must ensure their AI systems meet all relevant requirements before placing them on the market or putting them into service.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems placed on the market or put into service in the Union, including those established outside the Union
obligations.deadline: before placing on the market or putting into service

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall ensure continuous compliance with the AI Act during all phases of the AI lifecycle, including ongoing monitoring of and updates to AI systems placed on the market or put into service in the Union, to ensure that an AI system remains compliant and does not result in a practice prohibited under Article 5 AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market or put into service in the Union

obligations.requirement_text: Where a 'dual use' AI system is placed on the market, put into service, or used for both excluded purposes (military, defence, or national security) and non-excluded purposes (e.g. civilian or law enforcement), the provider shall ensure that the system complies with the requirements in the AI Act for those non-excluded purposes.
obligations.scope: Providers of dual-use AI systems placed on the market or put into service in the Union
obligations.evidence_required: Verification that the AI system complies with the AI Act before use for non-excluded purposes

obligations.requirement_text: Providers of 'dual use' AI systems (intended for both law enforcement/national security and other purposes) must ensure their compliance with the requirements in the AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems with dual use (including law enforcement and national security purposes)
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation demonstrating compliance with AI Act requirements for the dual-use system

obligations.requirement_text: Providers of AI systems released under free and open-source licences shall not benefit from the open-source exclusion if the AI system they place on the market or put into service constitutes a prohibited practice under Article 5 AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems released under free and open-source licences that are placed on the market or put into service as prohibited AI practices under Article 5

obligations.requirement_text: Each operator shall take measures for which they are best placed based on their role and control over the system in the value chain to ensure a responsible and safe provision and use of AI systems, balancing their risks and benefits.
obligations.scope: All operators (providers and deployers) of AI systems, whether intended-purpose or general-purpose

obligations.requirement_text: Deployers are expected not to use any AI system in a manner prohibited under Article 5 AI Act, including not to bypass any safety guardrails implemented by the providers of the system.
obligations.scope: Deployers of AI systems

obligations.requirement_text: Providers shall not place on the market or put into service AI systems, including general-purpose AI systems, that are reasonably likely to behave or be directly used in a manner prohibited by Article 5 AI Act, and are expected to take effective and verifiable measures to build in safeguards and prevent and mitigate such harmful behaviour and misuse to the extent they are reasonably foreseeable.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems, including general-purpose AI systems
obligations.evidence_required: Documented safeguards and measures (e.g., safe and ethical design, technical safeguards, restrictions of use, transparency and user control, instructions for use) integrated prior to placing on the market

obligations.requirement_text: Providers are expected to exclude use of their AI system for prohibited practices in their contractual relationships with deployers (i.e. in the terms of use of the AI system) and to provide appropriate information in the instructions of use for deployers regarding necessary human oversight.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems in their contractual relationships with deployers
obligations.evidence_required: Terms of use excluding prohibited practices; instructions of use containing appropriate information on human oversight

obligations.requirement_text: Providers shall take appropriate measures if they become aware that the system is misused for a specific prohibited purpose by specific deployers, including where such misuse is reported or the provider becomes otherwise aware.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems
obligations.evidence_required: Records of actions taken in response to identified or reported misuse

obligations.requirement_text: Member States must designate their competent market surveillance authorities by 2 August 2025.
obligations.scope: Member States
obligations.evidence_required: Official designation of competent market surveillance authorities

obligations.requirement_text: Where there are cross-border implications, the market surveillance authority of the Member State concerned must inform the Commission and the market surveillance authorities of other Member States where an AI system presents a risk at national level.
obligations.scope: Market surveillance authorities of Member States
obligations.evidence_required: Records of notifications to the Commission and other Member States' market surveillance authorities

obligations.requirement_text: Each Member State shall lay down rules if and to the extent that administrative fines may be imposed on public authorities and bodies established in that Member State as providers and deployers of AI systems.
obligations.scope: Member States
obligations.evidence_required: National legislation or rules specifying administrative fines applicable to public authorities and bodies

obligations.requirement_text: Deployers shall label 'deep fakes' and certain AI-generated text publications on matters of public interest.
obligations.scope: Deployers of AI systems generating deep fakes or AI-generated text on matters of public interest
obligations.evidence_required: Visible labelling applied to deep fakes and AI-generated text publications on matters of public interest

obligations.requirement_text: Providers shall ensure AI systems interacting with people are designed in a way to inform people that they are interacting with AI and not a human.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems that interact with natural persons
obligations.evidence_required: Design documentation and technical measures demonstrating that users are informed they are interacting with an AI system

obligations.requirement_text: Providers shall enable visible disclosure through design features embedded in the AI system, including technical measures enabling the detection of AI-generated and manipulated content.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems capable of generating or manipulating content
obligations.evidence_required: Technical documentation showing embedded design features and technical measures for detection of AI-generated and manipulated content

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(a) AI Act (as interpreted in section 3.2.3.c, paragraph 94)
obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems that deploy manipulative or deceptive techniques must implement appropriate preventive and mitigating measures to avoid or mitigate the risk of significant harms that are reasonably likely to result from such techniques.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems deploying subliminal, purposefully manipulative, or deceptive techniques
obligations.evidence_required: Records of preventive and mitigating measures implemented to avoid or reduce risk of significant harm

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(a) AI Act (as interpreted in section 3.2.3.c, paragraph 95, point 1)
obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems that deploy manipulative or deceptive techniques are encouraged to provide transparency in how the AI system operates, clear disclosures about its capabilities and limitations, and relevant information to ensure informed decisions; respect individual autonomy; and integrate appropriate user control and safeguard measures to ensure that the system is not deceptive and operates within the boundaries of lawful persuasion.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems deploying manipulative or deceptive techniques
obligations.evidence_required: Transparency documentation, user-facing disclosures, and records of user control and safeguard measures integrated into the system

obligations.requirement_text: The placing on the market, the putting into service or the use of an AI system that exploits any of the vulnerabilities of a natural person or a specific group of persons due to their age, disability or a specific social or economic situation, with the objective, or the effect, of materially distorting the behaviour of that person or a person belonging to that group in a manner that causes or is reasonably likely to cause that person or another person significant harm shall be prohibited.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems that exploit vulnerabilities of natural persons or specific groups due to age, disability, or specific social or economic situation

obligations.requirement_text: The placing on the market, the putting into service or the use of AI systems for the evaluation or classification of natural persons or groups of persons over a certain period of time based on their social behaviour or known, inferred or predicted personal or personality characteristics, with the social score leading to detrimental or unfavourable treatment in unrelated social contexts or unjustified or disproportionate treatment, shall be prohibited.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems used for social scoring of natural persons or groups of persons, in both public and private sectors.

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(c) AI Act (as analysed in section 4.2, paragraph 171)
obligations.requirement_text: In the case of checks by competent market surveillance authorities, the provider and the deployer of the AI system, each within their responsibilities, shall demonstrate that the AI practice is legitimate and justified, including by providing transparency of the functioning of the AI system, and information about the types of data and data sources, ensuring that only data related to the social context in which the score is used are processed for the purpose of the evaluation or classification, the system is performing as intended, and any resulting detrimental or unfavourable treatment is justified and proportionate to the social behaviour.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems used for evaluation or classification of persons (social scoring) subject to Article 5(1)(c) AI Act
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation demonstrating: (1) transparency of the AI system's functioning; (2) types of data and data sources used; (3) that only data related to the relevant social context is processed; (4) that the system performs as intended; (5) that any detrimental or unfavourable treatment is justified and proportionate to the social behaviour

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall carefully assess whether other applicable Union and national legislation applies to any particular AI scoring system used in their activities, in particular if there is more specific legislation that strictly regulates the types of data that can be used as relevant and necessary for specific evaluation purposes and if there are more specific rules and procedures to ensure justified and fair treatment.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems used for evaluation or classification of persons (social scoring)
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of legal assessment covering applicable Union and national legislation relevant to the AI scoring system

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall not place on the market, put into service, or use an AI system for making risk assessments of natural persons in order to assess or predict the risk of a natural person committing a criminal offence, based solely on the profiling of a natural person or on assessing their personality traits and characteristics, each within their respective responsibilities.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems used for individual risk assessment or crime prediction

obligations.requirement_text: AI systems used to assess or predict the risk of a person committing a criminal offence based solely on profiling of a natural person or on assessing their personality traits and characteristics shall be prohibited from being placed on the market, put into service, or used.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers (including law enforcement authorities, other public authorities, and private entities falling within the scope) of AI systems used for individual crime risk prediction

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(d) AI Act; Article 14 and Article 26 AI Act (as referenced in paragraph 204)
obligations.requirement_text: Where an AI system falls within the exclusion of Article 5(1)(d) AI Act (i.e., it supports human assessment based on objective and verifiable facts directly linked to criminal activity) and is classified as a high-risk AI system under Annex III, point 6(d), it shall be subject to the requirements and safeguards including human oversight; the human oversight must be assigned to persons with the necessary competence, training and authority who are able to properly understand the capabilities and limitations of the AI system, correctly interpret its output, address the risk of automation bias, and intervene or stop the use of the AI system if it does not perform as intended.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of high-risk AI systems (Annex III, point 6(d)) intended to be used by law enforcement authorities or on their behalf, where the system supports human assessment of involvement in criminal activity
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of human oversight procedures, training records, and competence/authority assignments for persons responsible for oversight; records demonstrating ability to intervene and stop the AI system

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(e) AI Act (as described in paragraphs 222–225)
obligations.requirement_text: The placing on the market, the putting into service for this specific purpose, or the use of AI systems that create or expand facial recognition databases through the untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV footage shall be prohibited.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems used for untargeted scraping of facial images to create or expand facial recognition databases

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(e) AI Act (as described in paragraph 230–231)
obligations.requirement_text: Where systems combine targeted searches for images or videos with untargeted searches, the untargeted scraping component is prohibited; scraping of the Internet or CCTV footage for the creation of a database step-by-step, selecting specific groups each time, shall fall within the prohibition where the end-result is functionally the same as pursuing untargeted scraping from the outset.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems that combine targeted and untargeted scraping of facial images for facial recognition databases

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(e) AI Act (as described in paragraph 236)
obligations.requirement_text: Existing facial databases built up prior to the entry into application of the prohibition, which are not further expanded through AI-enabled untargeted scraping, must comply with the applicable Union data protection rules.
obligations.scope: Any party (providers, deployers, or other operators) holding facial recognition databases created before the prohibition's entry into application
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of compliance with applicable Union data protection rules for pre-existing facial recognition databases

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems to infer emotions of a natural person in the areas of workplace and education institutions, except where the use of the AI system is intended for medical or safety reasons.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems that infer emotions in workplace or education institution contexts

obligations.requirement_text: Any use of emotion recognition systems for medical and safety reasons shall always remain limited to what is strictly necessary and proportionate, including limits in time, personal application and scale, and shall be accompanied by sufficient safeguards, such as prior written and motivated expert opinion relating to the specific use case.
obligations.scope: Employers and educational institutions (deployers) using emotion recognition AI systems under the medical or safety exception in workplace or education institution contexts
obligations.evidence_required: Prior written and motivated expert opinion relating to the specific use case; documentation of necessity and proportionality assessment

obligations.requirement_text: Employers and educators shall only deploy emotion recognition systems for medical and safety reasons in case of an explicit need, and data collected and processed in this context may not be used for any other purpose.
obligations.scope: Employers and educators (deployers) using emotion recognition AI systems under the medical or safety exception
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of the explicit need justifying deployment; records demonstrating data is not used for other purposes

obligations.requirement_text: Emotion recognition systems that classify as high-risk systems pursuant to Article 6(2) and Annex III(1)(c) AI Act shall comply with the high-risk requirements in Chapter III Section 2 AI Act and the transparency obligation of Article 50(3) AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of emotion recognition AI systems classified as high-risk under Article 6(2) and Annex III(1)(c)
obligations.evidence_required: Compliance documentation for Chapter III Section 2 high-risk requirements; records demonstrating fulfilment of Article 50(3) transparency obligation

obligations.requirement_text: Deployers of crowd-control or similar AI systems that may incidentally infer emotions of employees must employ safeguards to avoid the screening of employees and shall ensure that employees are not adversely affected by their use.
obligations.scope: Deployers of AI systems used in public-safety or crowd-control contexts that may incidentally infer employee emotions
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of safeguards implemented to prevent employee screening; records demonstrating employees are not adversely affected

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall not place on the market, put into service, or use biometric categorisation systems that categorise individually natural persons based on their biometric data to deduce or infer their race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious or philosophical beliefs, sex-life, or sexual orientation.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of biometric categorisation AI systems

obligations.requirement_text: AI systems that categorise natural persons based on their biometric data to deduce or infer their race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious or philosophical beliefs, sex-life or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of biometric categorisation AI systems
obligations.evidence_required: null

obligations.requirement_text: The use of 'real-time' remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for the purpose of law enforcement shall be prohibited, unless and in so far as such use is strictly necessary for one of the exhaustively listed objectives: (i) targeted search for victims of abduction, trafficking, or sexual exploitation, or missing persons; (ii) prevention of a specific, substantial and imminent threat to life or physical safety or a genuine and foreseeable threat of a terrorist attack; or (iii) localisation or identification of a person suspected of having committed a criminal offence listed in Annex II punishable by a custodial sentence of at least four years.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time remote biometric identification systems used in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
obligations.evidence_required: null

obligations.requirement_text: The use of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes under any of the three permitted objectives shall only be authorised where the conditions and safeguards of Article 5(2) to (7) AI Act are met, and only where national legislation permits such use.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time remote biometric identification systems used for law enforcement purposes (law enforcement authorities and entities acting on their behalf)
obligations.evidence_required: National legislation authorising use; documented compliance with conditions and safeguards of Article 5(2)–(7) AI Act

obligations.requirement_text: In the absence of national legislation allowing and regulating the use of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes, law enforcement authorities and entities acting on their behalf may not deploy such systems for law enforcement purposes.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities and entities acting on their behalf as deployers of real-time RBI systems
obligations.evidence_required: Evidence of applicable national legislation authorising use

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 6(2) and point (a) of Annex III AI Act
obligations.requirement_text: The placing on the market and the putting into service of real-time RBI systems, as well as the use of other RBI systems, shall be subject to the rules for high-risk AI systems in accordance with Article 6(2) and point (a) of Annex III AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of remote biometric identification AI systems (other than real-time use in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement, which is separately prohibited)
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation demonstrating compliance with high-risk AI system requirements under Chapter III of the AI Act

obligations.requirement_text: The retrospective (non-real-time) use of RBI systems for law enforcement purposes shall be subject to additional safeguards for the deployment of high-risk AI systems.
obligations.scope: Deployers of post/retrospective remote biometric identification systems used for law enforcement purposes
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of compliance with additional safeguards required by Article 26(10) AI Act for deployment of high-risk AI systems

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(h)(i) AI Act; Article 5(2)–(7) AI Act
obligations.requirement_text: Where real-time RBI systems are used in publicly accessible spaces for the targeted search of specific victims of abduction, trafficking in human beings, or sexual exploitation, or for the search for missing persons, the use shall be subject to strict necessity and the conditions and safeguards set out in Article 5(2)–(7) AI Act, including defining a perimeter of deployment and duration of use.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities and entities acting on their behalf as deployers of real-time RBI systems
obligations.evidence_required: Prior authorisation under national law; defined perimeter and duration of deployment; documented compliance with Article 5(2)–(7) safeguards

obligations.requirement_text: The use of 'real-time' remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for the purposes of law enforcement shall be deployed only to confirm the identity of the specifically targeted individual, and shall take into account: (a) the nature of the situation, in particular the seriousness, probability and scale of the harm if the system were not used; and (b) the consequences of the use of the system for the rights and freedoms of all persons concerned.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities deploying real-time remote biometric identification (RBI) systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act.
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation demonstrating that deployment was limited to confirming the identity of a specifically targeted individual and records of the assessment of harm and consequences to rights and freedoms.

obligations.requirement_text: The use of 'real-time' remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for the purposes of law enforcement shall comply with necessary and proportionate safeguards and conditions in relation to the use in accordance with the national law authorising the use thereof, in particular as regards the temporal, geographic and personal limitations.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities deploying real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act.
obligations.evidence_required: Records of national law authorisation with defined temporal, geographic and personal limitations.

obligations.requirement_text: The use of a 'real-time' remote biometric identification system in publicly accessible spaces shall be authorised only if the law enforcement authority has completed a fundamental rights impact assessment as provided for in Article 27 and has registered the system in the EU database according to Article 49. In duly justified cases of urgency, the use may be commenced without the registration in the EU database, provided that such registration is completed without undue delay.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities deploying real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act.
obligations.deadline: before deployment (or without undue delay in cases of urgency)
obligations.evidence_required: Completed Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA) per Article 27 AI Act; registration record in the EU database per Article 49 AI Act.

obligations.requirement_text: Deployers of real-time RBI systems must carry out a Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA) before the deployment of the authorised real-time RBI system. The FRIA must comply with the conditions laid down in Article 27 AI Act and shall include: a description of the RBI use and the deployer's processes; the period and frequency of use; the categories of persons and groups affected; the specific risks of harm to affected persons; human oversight measures; and risk mitigation measures.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems used in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act.
obligations.evidence_required: Documented FRIA covering: description of the RBI use and deployer processes, intended purpose, period and frequency of use, categories of affected persons, specific fundamental rights risks, human oversight procedures, and risk mitigation and redress measures.

obligations.requirement_text: The deployer of a real-time RBI system used in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes shall register the system in the EU database provided for in Article 49 AI Act before deployment. In cases of a duly justified emergency, deployment may start prior to registration, provided the law enforcement authority registers the system without undue delay.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act.
obligations.deadline: before deployment, or without undue delay in emergency cases
obligations.evidence_required: Registration record in the EU database (Article 49 AI Act); in emergency cases, documentation evidencing the justification for urgency and the timestamp of subsequent registration.

obligations.requirement_text: Each use for the purposes of law enforcement of a 'real-time' remote biometric identification system in publicly accessible spaces shall be subject to a prior authorisation granted by a judicial authority or an independent administrative authority whose decision is binding of the Member State in which the use is to take place, issued upon a reasoned request and in accordance with the detailed rules of national law. In a duly justified situation of urgency, the use may be commenced without an authorisation provided that such authorisation is requested without undue delay, at the latest within 24 hours.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities seeking to deploy real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act.
obligations.deadline: before deployment; in urgency, authorisation requested within 24 hours
obligations.evidence_required: Prior authorisation granted by judicial or independent administrative authority; reasoned request submitted; in urgency cases, evidence that authorisation was requested within 24 hours.

obligations.requirement_text: If the authorisation for the use of a real-time RBI system is rejected, the use shall be stopped with immediate effect and all the data, as well as the results and outputs of that use shall be immediately discarded and deleted.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities deploying real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act where authorisation has been rejected.
obligations.evidence_required: Records confirming cessation of use and deletion/discarding of all data, results and outputs upon rejection of authorisation.

obligations.requirement_text: No decision that would adversely affect an individual may be taken solely on the basis of the output of the real-time RBI system; automated decision-making based solely on the output of such a system which produces an adverse legal effect is prohibited.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities and any entities acting on their behalf deploying real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act.
obligations.evidence_required: Human oversight procedures documented in the FRIA, including records showing that no adverse decision was taken solely on the basis of the RBI system output.

obligations.requirement_text: Each use of a 'real-time' remote biometric identification system in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes shall be subject to a prior authorisation granted by a judicial authority or an independent administrative authority whose decision is binding of the Member State in which the use is to take place, issued upon a reasoned request and in accordance with the detailed rules of national law.
obligations.scope: Deployers (law enforcement authorities) of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces
obligations.deadline: before commencing each individual use (except in duly justified urgency situations)
obligations.evidence_required: Reasoned authorisation request and authorisation decision from competent judicial or independent administrative authority

obligations.requirement_text: In a duly justified situation of urgency where use is commenced without prior authorisation, such authorisation shall be requested without undue delay, at the latest within 24 hours.
obligations.scope: Deployers (law enforcement authorities) of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces
obligations.deadline: within 24 hours of commencing use in urgent situations
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of the urgent circumstances and evidence of timely submission of the authorisation request; logging of processing activities to substantiate timeliness

obligations.requirement_text: If an authorisation request in a case of urgency is rejected, the use of the real-time remote biometric identification system shall be stopped with immediate effect and all the data, as well as the results and outputs of that use, shall be immediately discarded and deleted.
obligations.scope: Deployers (law enforcement authorities) of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces
obligations.deadline: immediately upon rejection of the authorisation request
obligations.evidence_required: Records confirming cessation of use and deletion of all collected data, results, and outputs including reference databases built specifically for the contested search

obligations.requirement_text: No decision that produces an adverse legal effect on a person may be taken based solely on the output of the 'real-time' remote biometric identification system.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation showing that any adverse decision was supported by additional verification beyond the RBI system output

obligations.requirement_text: Each use of a 'real-time' remote biometric identification system in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes shall be notified to the relevant market surveillance authority and the national data protection authority in accordance with the national rules referred to in paragraph 5. The notification shall, as a minimum, contain the information specified under paragraph 6 and shall not include sensitive operational data.
obligations.scope: Deployers (law enforcement authorities) of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces
obligations.evidence_required: Notification records submitted to the relevant market surveillance authority and national data protection authority containing the minimum required information

obligations.requirement_text: Member States that adopt national laws authorising the use of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes shall notify those rules to the Commission at the latest 30 days following the adoption thereof.
obligations.scope: Member States that adopt national laws authorising use of real-time RBI systems for law enforcement in publicly accessible spaces
obligations.deadline: at the latest 30 days following the adoption of the national law
obligations.evidence_required: Notification submitted to the Commission of the adopted national law rules

obligations.requirement_text: National market surveillance authorities and the national data protection authorities of Member States that have been notified of the use of 'real-time' remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes pursuant to paragraph 4 shall submit to the Commission annual reports on such use.
obligations.scope: National market surveillance authorities and national data protection authorities of Member States
obligations.evidence_required: Annual reports submitted to the Commission based on the Commission-provided template, covering the number and results of authorisation decisions

obligations.requirement_text: The Commission shall publish annual reports on the use of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes, based on aggregated data in Member States on the basis of the annual reports referred to in paragraph 6. Those annual reports shall not include sensitive operational data of the related law enforcement activities.
obligations.scope: The European Commission
obligations.evidence_required: Published annual reports based on aggregated data from Member States, excluding sensitive operational data

obligations.requirement_text: No action or decision may be taken by the deployer of a real-time RBI system on the basis of the identification resulting from the system unless that identification has been separately verified and confirmed by at least two natural persons with the necessary competence, training and authority, or unless Union or national law considers the application of this requirement to be disproportionate.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time remote biometric identification systems (high-risk AI systems listed in Annex III, point 1(a))
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation showing that identification was verified and confirmed by at least two qualified natural persons before any action or decision was taken

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and users (deployers) of AI systems shall ensure a sufficient level of AI literacy of their staff and other persons dealing with the operation and use of AI systems, taking into account the persons on whom the systems are to be used.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems, including real-time RBI systems
obligations.evidence_required: Records of AI literacy measures and training provided to staff and persons dealing with the operation and use of AI systems

obligations.requirement_text: Article 5 AI Act applies as from 2 February 2025. The prohibitions in that provision shall apply to all AI systems regardless of whether they were placed on the market or put into service before or after that date.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of all AI systems subject to the prohibitions in Article 5 AI Act

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems must take necessary measures to ensure that they do not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that could constitute prohibited practices under Article 5 AI Act, even during the interim period before governance and penalties provisions become applicable.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems subject to the prohibitions in Article 5 AI Act
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of measures taken to assess and ensure AI systems do not constitute prohibited practices under Article 5 AI Act
